reasoning: The query asks about cherry blossom timing across all of Shikoku and unique sakura festivals. The tool was called for Tokushima only (not all four Shikoku prefectures) and returned zero festivals — no sakura data at all. The response includes a useful cherry-blossom query_intent block and advisory to try search_area, but delivers no actual festival or bloom-timing data. RULE A: the response content is the empty result, scored on what it provides. Single root cause: retrieval failure — Tokushima-only scope + zero festival matches.
missing_data: Sakura bloom timing for Shikoku; festivals in Kochi, Ehime, Kagawa prefectures; any named sakura meisho or local festivals across the region.
improvement_hint: Apply region fan-out for Shikoku (all 4 prefectures) when prefecture is Tokushima but query toponym is 'Shikoku'; also pipe the cherry_blossom concept through get_spots with kinds=[sakura_meisho_100,sakura_meisho] for bloom-site data.

reasoning: Query asks about mizubasho bloom season and summer wetland hiking highlights at Oze (尾瀬). The tool returned municipal scrape results for 片品村 — all about winter illuminations, a brand name, a zero-carbon committee, a taiko club, and a matsuri announcement — none of which address the mizubasho season or hiking viewpoints. There is one useful late-stage entry about a shuttle bus to Oze (大清水〜一ノ瀬), and a car-regulation notice, but these are incidental. The core bloom-season and wetland-highlight intent is entirely unmet. Root cause: retrieval failure — scrape results are administrative/event pages, not natural-heritage hiking content.
missing_data: Mizubasho bloom window (mid-May), Oze national park wetland highlights, hiking route descriptions, key viewpoints (Yamanohana, Ryugahama), access timing.
improvement_hint: Route 尾瀬 queries through search_hybrid or get_japan_heritage / national-park data; add a seasonal-event layer for mizubasho; filter out municipal administrative pages when query contains nature/hiking intent.

reasoning: Query is in Chinese asking about flower seasons in Okinawa specifically for camellia, kapok (木棉), and bougainvillea bloom periods. The response returns scrape results that contain '花' incidentally: a migration interview, a textile exhibition, a matsuri, a tea brand, and an American-restaurant feature — none of which discuss flowering seasons. RULE D does not apply since query_lang is zh and no language constraint was set. Root cause is retrieval failure: keyword '花' matched unrelated pages; bloom-season and specific flower-type data absent entirely.
missing_data: Camellia (山茶花) season Jan-Feb, Okinawa kapok / flame-of-the-forest timing, bougainvillea year-round but peak spring; any flower-garden or park spots in Okinawa.
improvement_hint: Detect flower-season intent and route to seasonal data layer; add bloom-calendar metadata for Okinawa flora; improve keyword filter to demote administrative/event scrape pages when query is nature/seasonal.

reasoning: Query asks about the 小樽雪あかりの路 festival dates and main candlelit venue locations. The canonical_festivals block returns exactly Q11431017 (Otaru Snow Light Path) with period '2月中旬 (約10日間)' and a description mentioning the canal and disused rail line — directly answering both sub-questions. The Sapporo Snow Festival and Asahikawa festival are also present as Hokkaido canonical festivals, which is contextually useful. One notable caveat: venue addresses/coordinates for the specific canal and rail-line sites are not included, which would help an agent confirm exact meeting points. Otherwise strong authoritative response.
missing_data: Specific venue names/coordinates for the main candlelit sites (運河会場, 手宮線会場); exact annual dates range.
improvement_hint: Add sub-venue coordinates for major canonical festivals so agents can surface precise location data without a follow-up get_spots call.

reasoning: Query is about a Tohoku Shinkansen-only trip asking which stations give easy access to sights. The tool returns Miyagi prefecture transport data with jr_pass_accessible flags and nearest stations — useful directional data. However the scope is Miyagi only; the broader Tohoku constraint (Iwate, Yamagata, Akita, Fukushima, Aomori) is not addressed. Also, many top-ranked spots list private operators (東北福祉大前 local line) rather than Shinkansen stops. The data is real and specific but covers only one of six Tohoku prefectures. Per RULE C: authoritative data with scope gap = 3 for affected dims. Single root cause: coverage failure — only one prefecture for a multi-prefecture query.
missing_data: Shinkansen-accessible stops in Iwate (Ichinoseki, Hiraizumi), Yamagata, Akita, Fukushima, Aomori prefectures; explicit Shinkansen station listing distinct from local lines.
improvement_hint: When query contains multi-prefecture region keyword (Tohoku/東北), apply region fan-out across all Tohoku prefectures; add a shinkansen_only filter flag to get_transport.

reasoning: Query specifically asks about Nagasaki city tram (路面電車) access to main sights. The response returns prefecture-wide transport hubs including Shimabara Castle (island ferry/private rail, far from city), Hirado Island (松浦鉄道, remote), and Hashima Island (no station at all) — mixing non-city sights with the city-center ones. The tram constraint is not reflected; most JR-accessible entries use JR Nagasaki Station as nearest, not specific tram stops. Dejima and Oura Church are present but nearest_station shows JR Nagasaki (long walk), not the tram stop (大浦天主堂 tram stop = ~5 min). Root cause: constraint failure — 路面電車 constraint not applied, prefecture-wide instead of city-focused.
missing_data: Tram line stop names for each city-center sight (大浦天主堂電停, 築町電停 etc.); city-only filtering; walk times from tram stops rather than JR station.
improvement_hint: Add tram-operator detection and tram-stop nearest-station matching; support municipality-scoped queries within get_transport; flag private island destinations as outside tram range.

reasoning: Query is about San-in Main Line stops in Tottori for JR Pass holders. The response returns Tottori prefecture hubs with jr_pass_accessible flags. Yonago (米子) and Tottori (鳥取) stations appear as nearest stations with JR西日本, which are San-in Main Line stops — relevant. However coverage includes only Tottori; the San-in Line runs through Shimane too. Also Utsubuki-tamagawa lists operator_name null with jr_pass_accessible false. Per RULE C: partial scope = 3. Root cause: coverage failure — single prefecture for a railway-corridor query spanning multiple prefectures.
missing_data: Shimane prefecture San-in stops (Matsue, Izumo, Masuda area); explicit San-in Line station sequence; note that Wakasa Castle is on a private line not San-in.
improvement_hint: Detect railway-corridor queries (San-in/山陰本線) and fan out across all prefectures the line traverses; add a jr_line filter option to get_transport.

reasoning: Query is in French asking specifically about a day-trip Kyoto→Amanohashidate (天橋立) by train feasibility. The response returns generic Kyoto prefecture transport hubs — Nijō Castle, Tō-ji, Nanzen-ji — almost all via non-JR city subway or private rail, with zero mention of Amanohashidate or the Kyoto Tango Railway line required to reach it. The specific destination (Amanohashidate, northern Kyoto) does not appear. RULE D applies for French language: constraint_handling -1 from its otherwise-deserved value. Root cause is constraint failure — neither the destination nor the travel-feasibility question is addressed. Also the response is in Japanese/English, not French.
missing_data: Amanohashidate station/access info; Kyoto Tango Railway route from Fukuchiyama; day-trip feasibility (approx 2h each way); French-language response.
improvement_hint: Detect named destination in query and prioritize that destination's transport entry; support destination-specific routing within get_transport; respond in requested lang=fr.

reasoning: Query is in Chinese asking about Osaka→Nara day trip by train and travel time. The tool returned Nara prefecture transport hubs in Japanese/English but not Chinese. Per RULE D: language mismatch (query zh, response ja/en) → constraint_handling -1. The data itself contains Kintetsu Nara, Hōryūji, Kōfuku-ji etc. with station info — useful for a Nara itinerary. reasoning: Query asks about limited-mobility tram+taxi access to Matsuyama sights without a car. The tool returns Matsuyama-scoped spots — 松山城, ロープウェー商店街, 石手寺 — which is correctly focused. However the mobility constraint is not handled: no tram stop names, no barrier-free information, no taxi-accessibility notes. 松山城 requires a ropeway/cable car to the hilltop (relevant for mobility), and the description even mentions バリアフリー化 for the 商店街, which is present in the scrape text. But the tool response doesn't synthesize mobility feasibility. Also get_spots was used rather than get_transport, so nearest_station data is absent for most scrape entries. Root cause: constraint failure — limited mobility not surfaced.

improvement_hint: Add mobility/accessibility flag detection; surface tram_stop field alongside nearest_station; include indoor_capable and barrier_free from existing fields in summaries.

reasoning: Query is in Korean asking whether a JR Pass can be used to circuit all 4 Shikoku prefectures by train. The response covers only Kochi prefecture, ignoring Tokushima, Ehime, Kagawa. reasoning: Query asks about Tokyo→Kamakura day trip by train and which stations are most efficient. The response returns Kanagawa prefecture hubs. reasoning: Query asks which Kyushu city is the best base for a train-only week without a car. reasoning: Query asks specifically about last-mile access to 白川郷 by bus, and when to transfer from train. The response returns Gifu prefecture hubs. 白川郷 (Q1002193) appears but has no nearest_station field (prominence_score 0.9, no station data). This is a critical gap for a last-mile access query. Other hubs like 飛騨萩原 on JR Takayama Line appear, which is contextually related to the Shirakawa-go corridor. But the bus transfer point (Kanazawa or Takayama) and the no-direct-rail-to-village fact are not in the data. Per RULE C: authoritative data with a scope gap = 3.

reasoning: Query asks about a no-car day trip from Hiroshima to Miyajima by ferry and train. The response returns 厳島神社 (Q191763) with nearest station 宮島ボートレース場駅 via 広島電鉄 (tram, 31 min walk to ferry pier area), and 厳島 (Q875301) with 前空駅 via JR West (jr_pass_accessible true, 41 min walk). 原爆ドーム and 平和記念公園 are present with nearby stations. The ferry is not explicitly named, but the combination of Hiroshima Peace Memorial sites + Itsukushima entries gives an agent enough to construct the day-trip narrative. One notable caveat: the tram/Miyajima ferry connection is implicit rather than explicit, and walk times to the ferry pier are somewhat long. Good overall.

improvement_hint: Add ferry_accessible flag and ferry operator info for island destinations; note JR Pass coverage of JR ferry services.

reasoning: Query is in Indonesian asking about visiting Nikko without a rental car using JR Pass and bus. Response is in Japanese/English. reasoning: Query asks about visiting Tokaido post towns (宿場町) by local train from Shizuoka on JR Pass. reasoning: Query asks how to reach Aso volcano from Kumamoto station without a car using buses or trains. The response includes 阿蘇神社 (Q715639) with nearest station 宮地駅 via JR Kyushu (jr_pass_accessible true, 15 min walk) — the JR Hohi Line runs from Kumamoto to Miyaji for Aso area. This is genuinely useful data. However 阿蘇山 / the volcano crater itself does not appear with station data, and the bus from Miyaji to the crater (which is necessary) is absent. 通潤橋 (Q3077059) and 人吉城 (Q8011484) have no station data either. The Aso Shrine entry gives a partial answer but not the full crater-access chain.

improvement_hint: Add 阿蘇山 / Aso caldera as a named hub with bus_accessible info; include bus operator and frequency for last-mile volcano access.

reasoning: Query is in Chinese asking about Osaka→Koya-san without car using JR Pass + cable car. Response is in Japanese/English. RULE D: lang=zh but response in ja/en → constraint_handling -1. 金剛峯寺 (Q1203429) appears with nearest station 極楽橋駅 via 南海電気鉄道 (private, jr_pass_accessible false) — correctly flags that JR Pass is not valid for the Nankai route, which is an important constraint answer. missing_data: Chinese-language response; Nankai Koya Line + Koyasan Cable Car route description; note that JR Pass does NOT cover Nankai to Koya-san; alternative via JR Wakayama then bus.

reasoning: Query asks about a Sendai→Matsushima day trip by train only. The response includes 松島 (Q1144855) with 松島海岸駅 via JR East (jr_pass_accessible true, 26 min walk) — directly answering the train question. Sendai-area spots (大崎八幡宮, 陸奥国分寺 via local lines) provide context. The 26-min walk from Matsushima-Kaigan Station to the bay area is a slight caveat (it's actually closer to 5-10 min to the waterfront), but Wikidata coordinates may place it at a specific inland point. Multiple JR-accessible hubs confirm feasibility. One notable caveat: the response doesn't explicitly confirm one day is enough, though the proximity data (Matsushima is ~40 min by train from Sendai) enables an agent to draw that conclusion.
missing_data: Approximate train travel time from Sendai to Matsushima-Kaigan (approx 40 min on JR Senseki Line); confirmation that one day is sufficient.
improvement_hint: Add inter-city approximate travel-time field between major hubs; clarify walk_minutes is from station to Wikidata centroid, not to the nearest tourist landmark.
